behaviors.description: The embedding vector produced for a given query string has the correct dimensionality for the vector database index.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: the failure is at the embedding-model-to-index boundary (model output dimension vs index schema), not just local math. REAL-INFRA: a fake embedder can miss provider/model-version dimension drift. LLM-DEP: this behavior validates a real AI/ML call output property (dimension), so a stub can hide real failures. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Integration because behavior text requires the vector is actually "produced" for a query, which is disqualified by stub-only tests.
behaviors.test_description: Low (Integration): setup stub embedder + index dim check; assert len(vector)==index_dim; catches wiring bug (wrong field mapped); misses real provider dimension drift. Current (Agentic): setup real embedding model configured in prod + real index metadata; assert produced vector length equals index dimension; catches model/version mismatch and misconfigured embedding endpoint. High (Workflow): setup full query->embed->search->rerank pipeline; assert no dimension errors end-to-end; catches pipeline regressions; misses little beyond Agentic for this specific behavior.

behaviors.description: The similarity search returns results ordered by descending cosine similarity score.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure appears at module handoff (query vector -> vector store query params -> score mapping/order). REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network/subprocess is strictly required if using a local/in-process vector store fixture. LLM-DEP: correctness does not depend on LLM judgment. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no required real OS/network, (b) no AI output-quality dependency, (c) no multi-agentic chain.
behaviors.test_description: Low (Unit): setup pure sorter function with fixed scored items; assert descending order; catches local comparator bug; misses DB/client score-field mapping issues. Current (Integration): setup local vector index with known vectors and query through repository/service modules; assert returned scores are monotonic descending and IDs match expected rank; catches wrong sort direction, wrong score field, or post-processing reorder bug. High (System): setup real remote vector DB over HTTP; assert same ordering contract; catches transport/protocol issues; mostly unnecessary for this behavior’s stated failure mode.

behaviors.description: The LLM re-ranking step changes the order of at least some results compared to the raw similarity ranking when the query has nuanced intent.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: with mocked reranker, order change can be forced and always pass. LLM-DEP: behavior explicitly depends on model judgment for nuanced intent; this is exactly non-deterministic AI output quality. MULTI-STEP: only one agentic decision point is required for the assertion, so not Workflow. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Integration because behavior text requires reranking to occur specifically "when the query has nuanced intent," which a deterministic stub cannot validate.
behaviors.test_description: Low (Integration): setup stub reranker that swaps two items; assert order differs from raw ranking; catches wiring bugs (reranker not invoked); misses whether real LLM understands nuance. Current (Agentic): setup curated nuanced queries + candidate lists, call real LLM reranker repeatedly with fixed prompt; assert at least one meaningful reorder vs baseline within acceptance threshold; catches prompt regressions and degraded semantic reranking. High (Workflow): setup multi-turn planner/tool/critic pipeline around reranking; assert downstream decisions improve; catches cross-agent effects; beyond this behavior’s scope.

behaviors.description: A query with no close matches in the database returns an empty result set rather than low-relevance noise.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure usually occurs where similarity scores, threshold policy, and result filtering interact across modules. REAL-INFRA: can be reproduced with local fixtures and in-process DB/index; no mandatory real OS/network. LLM-DEP: this behavior is about thresholding/noise suppression, not LLM judgment quality. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no required OS/network/subprocess, (b) no required real AI output quality, (c) no multi-agentic sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Low (Unit): setup filter function with synthetic scores below threshold; assert empty list; catches threshold comparator bug; misses score-scale mismatch from search layer. Current (Integration): setup local index with intentionally distant vectors and service threshold config; run query path through embedding adapter (can be deterministic fixture) + search + filter; assert empty results; catches normalization/threshold handoff bugs that leak noise. High (System): setup production-like remote vector DB and network path; assert empties for no-match corpus; catches infra-specific behavior but not required for stated failure.

behaviors.description: The end-to-end latency from receiving the query to returning re-ranked results stays below 2 seconds for the median case.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: latency behavior depends on real network/API/subprocess/runtime effects; mocks hide queueing, connection, serialization, and external service latency. MOCK-HIDE: mocked embedding/LLM/vector calls can always satisfy <2s and miss real timeout/slowness failures. LLM-DEP: output content quality is not under test, so Agentic is not strictly required. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Agentic because behavior text targets timing only ("latency ... below 2 seconds"), not model decision correctness.
behaviors.test_description: Low (Integration): setup in-process stubs for embed/search/rerank and timer around handler; assert p50<2s; catches local algorithmic slowness; misses network and external API latency. Current (System): setup production-like deployment path with real HTTP request handling and real downstream services (or faithful staging equivalents), run load sample and compute median E2E latency; assert p50<2s; catches real infra latency regressions. High (Workflow): setup multi-agent benchmark across chained agentic stages and replans; assert workflow SLA; catches broader pipeline issues but exceeds this single-request API behavior.
